commands.review_summary:
The PR disables Codex app-server native Code Mode, user MCP, and app-backed plugin execution during active OpenClaw sandboxes, exposes `sandbox_exec`/`sandbox_process`, updates Codex sandbox/context tests and docs, and omits an update compatibility env var when no candidate version is available.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main maps active OpenClaw sandbox state into Codex host-side `workspaceWrite` policy, matching the linked Docker canary report. I did not rerun the before-fix Docker canary in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The protected `maintainer` label and deliberate compatibility/security-boundary change require human approval; I found no narrow ClawSweeper repair finding.

target.title: Fail closed Codex native execution in OpenClaw sandboxes
target.body:
## Summary

- Disable Codex app-server native Code Mode/user MCP/app-backed execution whenever an OpenClaw sandbox is active.
- Route sandboxed Codex shell access through OpenClaw-owned `sandbox_exec` / `sandbox_process` dynamic tools instead.
- Reproject bootstrap context for native-disabled transient Codex threads and document the active-sandbox behavior.

Fixes #83796

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`
- `$autoreview` fallback reviewer, followed by rejected/non-actionable finding review
- AWS Crabbox fix proof: provider `aws`, run `run_bde5bfcd55db`, lease `cbx_49a348abca7b`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server native shell/code execution no longer runs on the Gateway host while an OpenClaw sandbox is active; sandboxed shell access is exposed as OpenClaw `sandbox_exec` / `sandbox_process` instead.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux host with real Docker sandbox image `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim`; provider `aws`, run `run_bde5bfcd55db`, lease `cbx_49a348abca7b`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Installed dependencies, built `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim`, started the Codex app-server attempt path with `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode=all`, and issued an app-server `item/tool/call` for `sandbox_exec`.

Evidence after fix: The app-server `thread/start` config had `features.code_mode=false`, `features.code_mode_only=false`, and `environments=[]`; registered dynamic tools included `sandbox_exec` and `sandbox_process`.

Observed result after fix: `sandbox_exec` returned the proof token, a host-only canary in `/tmp` remained present and unread by the command, no host marker was written, and the proof marker was found inside Docker container `openclaw-sbx-agent-proof-codex-sandbox-fix-f11279bd`.

What was not tested: A live OpenAI model-driven tool-selection turn was not used for the final proof; the proof made the app-server protocol tool request directly so it was deterministic. A prior full Gateway proof harness attempt hung before reaching the Codex app-server path and was not counted as proof.
target.author: joshavant
